title: bet
description: Automated brain extraction tool for FSL

fractional_intensity.description: Fractional intensity threshold (0->1); default=0.5; smaller values give larger brain outline estimates
fractional_intensity.title: Fractional intensity threshold

vg_fractional_intensity.description: Vertical gradient in fractional intensity threshold (-1->1); default=0; positive values give larger brain outline at bottom, smaller at top
vg_fractional_intensity.title: Vertical gradient fractional intensity threshold

center_of_gravity.description: The xyz coordinates of the center of gravity (voxels, not mm) of initial mesh surface. Must have exactly three numerical entries in the list (3-vector).
center_of_gravity.title: Center of gravity vector

overlay.description: Generate brain surface outline overlaid onto original image
overlay.title: Overlay flag

approx_skull.description: Generate rough skull image (not as clean as betsurf)
approx_skull.title: Approximate skull flag

no_seg_output.description: Don't generate segmented brain image output
no_seg_output.title: No segmented brain image flag

vtk_mesh.description: Generate brain surface as mesh in .vtk format
vtk_mesh.title: VTK format brain surface mesh flag

head_radius.description: head radius (mm not voxels); initial surface sphere is set to half of this
head_radius.title: Head Radius

thresholding.description: Apply thresholding to segmented brain image and mask
thresholding.title: Threshold segmented image flag

robust_iters.description: More robust brain center estimation, by iterating BET with a changing center-of-gravity.
robust_iters.title: Robust iterations flag

residual_optic_cleanup.description: This attempts to cleanup residual eye and optic nerve voxels which bet2 can sometimes leave behind. This can be useful when running SIENA or SIENAX, for example. Various stages involving standard-space masking, morphpological operations and thresholdings are combined to produce a result which can often give better results than just running bet2.
residual_optic_cleanup.title: Residual optic cleanup flag

reduce_bias.description: This attempts to reduce image bias, and residual neck voxels. This can be useful when running SIENA or SIENAX, for example. Various stages involving FAST segmentation-based bias field removal and standard-space masking are combined to produce a result which can often give better results than just running bet2.
reduce_bias.title: Bias reduction flag

slice_padding.description: This can improve the brain extraction if only a few slices are present in the data (i.e., a small field of view in the Z direction). This is achieved by padding the end slices in both directions, copying the end slices several times, running bet2 and then removing the added slices.
slice_padding.title: Slice padding flag

whole_set_mask.description: This option uses bet2 to determine a brain mask on the basis of the first volume in a 4D data set, and applies this to the whole data set. This is principally intended for use on FMRI data, for example to remove eyeballs. Because it is normally important (in this application) that masking be liberal (ie that there be little risk of cutting out valid brain voxels) the -f threshold is reduced to 0.3, and also the brain mask is "dilated" slightly before being used.
whole_set_mask.title: Mask-whole-set flag

additional_surfaces.description: This runs both bet2 and betsurf programs in order to get the additional skull and scalp surfaces created by betsurf. This involves registering to standard space in order to allow betsurf to find the standard space masks it needs.
additional_surfaces.title: Additional surfaces flag

additional_surfaces_t2.description: This is the same as -A except that a T2 image is also input, to further improve the estimated skull and scalp surfaces. As well as carrying out the standard space registration this also registers the T2 to the T1 input image.
additional_surfaces_t2.title: Additional surfaces with T2
